p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future William de Vescy sends greeting.

to have granted and by this my charter to have confirmed, moved by love of God and for the salvation of my soul and for the salvation of all my ancestors and successors, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God, the two ploughgates of land, one of which Patrick of Ryedale gave to them, and the other Walter his son, in the territory of Lilliesleaf, with the pastures belonging to them, by the same boundaries which are contained in the charters of the aforesaid Patrick and Walter his son. And I will and grant that the aforesaid monks have and hold the aforesaid lands and pastures in free, pure and perpetual alms, with all their easements and liberties, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitely, fully and honourably in the kingdom of Scotland, reserving to myself and my heirs the service pertaining to those lands from the aforesaid Patrick and Walter and their heirs. I and my heirs also maintain and warrant all things aforesaid to the aforesaid monks, that they have and hold and use them by all means which seem best and useful to them, just as the charters of the aforesaid donors and confirmations of Margery my mother which the aforesaid monks have thereupon bear witness.
With these witnesses, the lord J(ohn) of Maxwell, Robert of Roos, Waldef the marischal, William the latimer (interpreter), Ralph Bardolf, Simon of Horsley, Robert of Airth, and many others.
